lede: A look at March 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including John Major and Jennifer Capriati, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Same-sex marriage in the United Kingdom
events.desc: The first same-sex marriages in England and Wales are performed.

events.title: Bulgaria
events.desc: Bulgaria, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Romania, Slovakia, and Slovenia join NATO as full members.

events.title: 1999 Chamoli earthquake
events.desc: A magnitude 6.8 earthquake in India strikes the Chamoli district in Uttar Pradesh, killing 103.

events.title: NASA
events.desc: NASA's Mariner 10 becomes the first space probe to fly by Mercury.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: The last United States combat soldiers leave South Vietnam.

events.title: Funeral of Yuri Gagarin and Vladimir Seryogin
events.desc: The funeral of Yuri Gagarin, the first man in space, begins in Moscow, with thousands of people in attendance.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: British Royal Navy and Royal Australian Navy forces defeat those of the Italian Regia Marina off the Peloponnesian coast of Greece in the Battle of Cape Matapan.

events.title: North American Regional Broadcasting Agreement
events.desc: The North American Regional Broadcasting Agreement goes into effect at 03:00 local time.

events.title: Knights of Columbus
events.desc: The Knights of Columbus is established.

events.title: Mexican–American War
events.desc: Mexican–American War: United States forces led by General Winfield Scott take Veracruz after a siege.

births.name: John Major
births.line: English banker and politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

births.name: John Tyler
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 10th President of the United States

births.name: John McEwen
births.line: Australian farmer and politician, 18th Prime Minister of Australia

deaths.text: Nicolaus Bruhns, Danish-German organist, violinist, and composer
